subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Advantage of linked list over array:
options: Dynamic size, O(1) insertion at head
explanation: Linked lists grow dynamically. Insertion at head is O(1). Arrays need contiguous memory.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Time to access kth element in singly linked list:
explanation: Must traverse from head: O(k) sequential access. No random access.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Cycle detection in linked list uses:
explanation: Slow pointer moves 1 step, fast moves 2. If they meet → cycle exists. O(n) time, O(1) space.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Doubly linked list has:
options: Pointers to both next and previous nodes
explanation: DLL: each node has prev and next pointers. Allows bidirectional traversal.

subject: Programming, DS & Algorithms
question: Reversing a singly linked list in-place is:
explanation: Iterate through, reversing pointers one by one. Single pass: O(n) time, O(1) extra space.
